title: ECONOMICS QUIZ TOPIC BASICS OF ECONOMY Quiz grp httpst me

questions.question: Which one of the following central features is not associated with Capitalist Economy? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सी, पूँजीवादी अर्थव्यवस्था से संबद्ध मुख्य विशेषताओं में शामिल नहीं है? CDS - II - 2022
options: There is generalised commodity production — it has market value. / इसका सामान्यीकृत पण्य उत्पादन होता है - इसका बाज़ार मूल्य होता है।
Productive wealth is held predominantly in private hands. / उत्पादक संपत्ति प्रधान रूप से निजी हाथों में होती है।
Economic life is organised according to market principles. / बाज़ार सिद्धांतों के अनुसार उपयोगिता काल व्यवस्थित किया जाता है।
Economic organisation is based on planning, a supposedly rational process of resource allocation. / तार्किक संगठन, संसाधन आबंटन की कल्पित तर्कसंगत प्रक्रिया वाली योजना पर आधारित होता है।
questions.explanation: Central planning and resource allocation by a governing authority are hallmarks of a Socialist or Command Economy. In contrast, a Capitalist (or Market) Economy relies on market forces of supply and demand, with minimal government intervention, a concept known as 'Laissez-faire'.

questions.question: With reference to the sectors of the Indian economy, consider the following pairs: / भारतीय अर्थव्यवस्था के क्षेत्रों के संदर्भ में, निम्नलिखित युग्मों पर विचार कीजिए: CSE - 2024 1. Storage of Agricultural produce - Secondary / कृषि उत्पाद का भंडारण - द्वितीयक 2. Dairy farm - Primary / डेरी फार्म - प्राथमिक 3. Mineral exploration - Tertiary / खनिज की खोज - तृतीयक 4. Weaving cloth - Secondary / कपड़ा की बुनाई - द्वितीयक Q2. How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched? / उपर्युक्त युग्मों में से कितने सही सुमेलित हैं?
questions.explanation: Correct pairs are 2 and 4. Dairy farming (2) is part of the Primary sector (extraction from nature). Weaving cloth (4) is a manufacturing activity, part of the Secondary sector. Storage (1) is a service, hence Tertiary. Mineral exploration (3) is extraction, hence Primary.

questions.question: Which of the following activities constitute the real sector in the economy? / निम्नलिखित में कौन-कौन से कार्यकलाप अर्थव्यवस्था में वास्तविक क्षेत्रक (रियल सेक्टर) का निर्माण करते हैं? CSE - 2022 1. Farmers harvesting their crops / किसानों का अपनी फसलें काटना 2. Textile mills converting raw cotton into fabrics / कपड़ा मिलों का कच्चे कपास को कपड़े में बदलना 3. A commercial bank lending money to a trading company / किसी वाणिज्यिक बैंक का किसी व्यापार कंपनी को धनराशि उधार देना 4. A corporate body issuing Rupee Denominated Bonds overseas / किसी कॉर्पोरेट निकाय का विदेश में रुपये-अंकित मूल्य के बांड जारी करना Q3. Select the correct answer using the code given below: / नीचे दिए कूट का प्रयोग कर सही उत्तर चुनिए:
questions.explanation: The real sector refers to the part of the economy concerned with producing actual goods and services. Farming (1) and manufacturing (2) are core real sector activities. Lending money and issuing bonds (3, 4) are part of the financial sector, which facilitates but does not directly produce goods/services.

questions.question: Which of the following statement is correct regarding Public Goods? / सार्वजनिक वस्तुओं के संबंध में निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा कथन सही है? SSC CHSL 2025
options: Clothes are an example of Public good. / कपड़े सार्वजनिक वस्तु का एक उदाहरण हैं।
It can be provided through the market mechanism. / ये बाज़ार तंत्र के माध्यम से उपलब्ध कराए जा सकते हैं।
It can be provided by the transactions between individual consumers and producers. / ये व्यक्तिगत उपभोक्ताओं और उत्पादकों के बीच लेन-देन द्वारा उपलब्ध कराए जा सकते हैं।
Generally, the benefits of public goods are available to all. / सामान्यतः, सार्वजनिक वस्तुओं के लाभ सभी को प्राप्त होते हैं।
questions.explanation: The key characteristic of a public good is that it is non-excludable, meaning its benefits are available to everyone, regardless of whether they pay for it. Clothes are private goods as they are both excludable and rivalrous.

questions.question: Which of the following statements shows an accurate understanding of a macroeconomic concept? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा कथन एक व्यापक आर्थिक अवधारणा की सटीक समझ दर्शाता है? SSC CPO - 2025
options: Gross Domestic Product includes earnings sent home by citizens working abroad. / सकल घरेलू उत्पाद में विदेशों में काम करने वाले नागरिकों द्वारा घर भेजे गए आय शामिल होते हैं।
When the general price level rises, the value of money tends to fall. / जब सामान्य मूल्य स्तर बढ़ता है, तो मुद्रा का मूल्य गिरने लगता है।
A country's budget is considered balanced when exports exceed imports. / किसी देश का बजट संतुलित तब माना जाता है जब निर्यात आयात से अधिक हो।
The central bank controls inflation mainly through changes in income tax rates. / केंद्रीय बैंक मुख्य रूप से आयकर दरों में परिवर्तन के माध्यम से मुद्रास्फीति को नियंत्रित करते हैं।
questions.explanation: Inflation (a rise in the general price level) means that each unit of currency buys fewer goods and services; consequently, the value or purchasing power of money falls. Remittances from abroad are part of GNP, not GDP. A balanced budget relates to government spending and revenue, not trade. Central banks use monetary policy (like interest rates), not fiscal policy (like taxes), to control inflation.

questions.question: Which of the following best reflects the change in sector importance in India over time? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा विकल्प भारत में समय के साथ क्षेत्र के महत्व में आए परिवर्तन को सबसे अच्छी तरह दर्शाता है? RRB NTPC 16/06/2025
options: Tertiary → Secondary → Primary / तृतीयक → माध्यमिक → प्राथमिक
Tertiary → Primary → Secondary / तृतीयक → प्राथमिक → माध्यमिक
Secondary → Primary → Tertiary / माध्यमिक → प्राथमिक → तृतीयक
Primary → Secondary → Tertiary / प्राथमिक → माध्यमिक → तृतीयक
questions.explanation: This represents the typical path of economic development. Economies tend to evolve from being dominated by the primary (agricultural) sector, to the secondary (industrial) sector, and finally to the tertiary (service) sector as they mature and develop.

questions.question: Among the following countries, where did the idea of 'Social Market Economy' emerge in the 1950s? / निम्नलिखित में से किस देश में 1950 के दशक में 'सामाजिक बाज़ार अर्थव्यवस्था (सोशल मार्केट इकोनॉमी)' का विचार सामने आया?
options: United States of America / संयुक्त राज्य अमेरिका
questions.explanation: The Social Market Economy model, also known as Rhine capitalism, originated in post-WWII West Germany. It combines a capitalist free-market system with social policies and government regulation to establish fair competition and a welfare state, aiming for a "third way" between laissez-faire capitalism and socialism.

questions.question: The activities or services focussed on creation, rearrangement and interpretation of new and existing ideas are referred to as? / नवीन और वर्तमान विचारों की रचना, पुनर्गठन और व्याख्या पर केन्द्रित क्रियाकलापों अथवा सेवाओं को क्या कहते हैं?
questions.explanation: The Quinary sector involves the highest levels of decision-making in an economy. This includes top executives in government, science, universities, non-profits, healthcare, culture, and the media. It is focused on the creation and interpretation of new ideas and policies, often called "gold collar" professions.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a "public good"? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा "सार्वजनिक वस्तु" का उदाहरण है?
questions.explanation: A public good is both non-excludable (people can't be prevented from using it) and non-rivalrous (one person's use doesn't reduce its availability to others). Police services fit this; everyone benefits from their presence. Transport and electricity are excludable (require payment). Private parks are excludable by definition.

questions.question: Under the 'Laissez-faire' economic theory, what is the role of the government? / 'लेसेज-फेयर' इकोनॉमिक थ्योरी के तहत सरकार की क्या भूमिका है?
options: Total control of the economy / अर्थव्यवस्था का पूर्ण नियंत्रण
Minimum interference in economic affairs / आर्थिक मामलों में न्यूनतम हस्तक्षेप
Setting prices for all goods / सभी वस्तुओं के लिए कीमतें निर्धारित करना
Owning all industries / सभी उद्योगों का स्वामित्व
questions.explanation: 'Laissez-faire' is a French term meaning 'let do' or 'leave alone.' It is the core principle of a free-market capitalist economy, advocating for minimal government interference in economic activities, allowing the "invisible hand" of the market to regulate itself.

questions.question: What term refers to the process of rapid integration or interconnection between countries due to greater foreign investment, trade, and movement of goods, services, and technology? / विदेशी निवेश, व्यापार और वस्तुओं, सेवाओं और प्रौद्योगिकी के आवागमन में वृद्धि के कारण देशों के बीच तीव्र एकीकरण या अंतर्संबंध की प्रक्रिया को किस शब्द से संदर्भित किया जाता है?
questions.explanation: Globalization is the process of interaction and integration among people, companies, and governments worldwide. It is primarily driven by international trade, investment, and aided by information technology, leading to increased movement of goods, services, capital, and labor across borders.

questions.question: Which economic concept is known as the 'invisible hand'? / किस आर्थिक अवधारणा को 'अदृश्य हाथ' के नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: The "invisible hand" is a metaphor introduced by economist Adam Smith in his book 'The Wealth of Nations'. It describes the unintended greater social benefits and public good brought about by individuals acting in their own self-interest within a free market. These self-regulating market forces (supply and demand) guide the economy.

questions.question: What was a key feature of the barter system that made it less efficient compared to using money? / वस्तु विनिमय प्रणाली की वह कौन सी प्रमुख विशेषता थी जिसके कारण यह मुद्रा के उपयोग की तुलना में कम कुशल थी?
options: Double Coincidence of Wants / इच्छाओं का दोहरा संयोग
Lack of Trade / व्यापार का अभाव
questions.explanation: The barter system requires a "double coincidence of wants," meaning two parties each must have a good the other wants for a direct exchange to occur. This is often difficult to find, making trade inefficient. Money solves this by acting as a universally accepted medium of exchange.

questions.question: Choose the correct statement regarding public goods. / सार्वजनिक वस्तुओं के संबंध में सही कथन का चयन करें।
options: They are non-excludable and non-rival. / वे अपवर्जनीय और गैर-प्रतिस्पर्धी हैं।
They are excludable and rival. / वे अपवर्जनीय और प्रतिस्पर्धी हैं।
They are excludable but non-rival. / वे अपवर्जनीय हैं लेकिन गैर-प्रतिस्पर्धी हैं।
They are non-excludable but rival. / वे अपवर्जनीय हैं लेकिन प्रतिस्पर्धी हैं।
questions.explanation: Public goods are defined by two key characteristics: they are non-excludable, meaning it's not feasible to prevent anyone from consuming the good, and they are non-rivalrous, meaning one person's consumption does not reduce the amount available for others. Examples include national defense and fresh air.

questions.question: In economics, what does 'opportunity cost' refer to? / अर्थशास्त्र में, "अवसर लागत" किसे संदर्भित करती है?
options: The next best alternative forgone / अगला सर्वोत्तम विकल्प जो छूट गया
The total money spent / कुल खर्च की गई राशि
A discount offered / एक छूट की पेशकश
A tax collected / एकत्रित किया गया कर
questions.explanation: Opportunity cost represents the potential benefits an individual, investor, or business misses out on when choosing one alternative over another. It is the value of the "next-best" alternative that was not chosen.

questions.question: Software development and scientific research are activities associated with which sector of the economy? / सॉफ्टवेयर विकास और वैज्ञानिक अनुसंधान अर्थव्यवस्था के किस क्षेत्र से संबंधित गतिविधियाँ हैं?
questions.explanation: The Quaternary sector is the knowledge-based part of the economy, which typically includes services such as information technology, information-generation and -sharing, media, and research and development. It is sometimes seen as a sub-category of the Tertiary sector.

questions.question: Which of the following is considered a key focus of macroeconomics? / निम्नलिखित में से किसे मैक्रोइकॉनॉमिक्स का प्रमुख केंद्र माना जाता है?
options: The behavior of individual consumers / व्यक्तिगत उपभोक्ताओं का व्यवहार
The allocation of resources in a specific market / किसी विशिष्ट बाज़ार में संसाधनों का आवंटन
The overall performance and behavior of the economy / अर्थव्यवस्था का समग्र प्रदर्शन और व्यवहार
The study of firm-specific production decisions / फर्म-विशिष्ट उत्पादन निर्णयों का अध्ययन
questions.explanation: Macroeconomics is the branch of economics that studies the behavior and performance of an economy as a whole. It focuses on aggregate changes such as unemployment, growth rate, gross domestic product (GDP), and inflation.

questions.question: What are the four main 'factors of production' in economics? / अर्थशास्त्र में उत्पादन के चार मुख्य 'कारक' कौन से हैं?
options: Land, Labor, Capital, and Entrepreneurship / भूमि, श्रम, पूंजी और उद्यमिता
Money, Machines, Land, and People / पैसा, मशीनें, जमीन और लोग
Taxes, Loans, Goods, and Services / कर, ऋण, वस्तुएं और सेवाएं
Primary, Secondary, Tertiary, and Quaternary / प्राथमिक, द्वितीयक, तृतीयक और चतुर्थक
questions.explanation: The factors of production are the inputs needed for the creation of a good or service. These are land (natural resources), labor (human effort), capital (man-made resources like machinery), and entrepreneurship (the individual who combines the other three to earn a profit).

questions.question: A steel manufacturing plant belongs to which sector of the economy? / इस्पात निर्माण संयंत्र अर्थव्यवस्था के किस क्षेत्र में आता है? DP - 2025
questions.explanation: The secondary sector involves the transformation of raw materials (from the primary sector) into finished or manufactured goods. A steel plant takes iron ore (primary) and manufactures steel (secondary), making it part of the secondary/industrial sector.

questions.question: Which economic sector is commonly referred to as the 'industrial sector'? / किस आर्थिक क्षेत्र को आमतौर पर 'औद्योगिक क्षेत्र' कहा जाता है? DP - 2025
questions.explanation: The secondary sector is also known as the industrial sector because it includes all industries that produce a finished, usable product or are involved in construction. This encompasses manufacturing, processing, and construction.

questions.question: What does the term 'public goods' refer to? / 'सार्वजनिक वस्तुएं' शब्द से क्या तात्पर्य है? DP - 2025
options: Goods produced only by government / केवल सरकार द्वारा उत्पादित वस्तुएँ
Goods that are excludable / बहिष्करणीय वस्तुएँ
Goods accessible to all and non-rival / सभी के लिए सुलभ और गैर-प्रतिस्पर्धी वस्तुएँ
Goods exported abroad / विदेशों में निर्यात की जाने वाली वस्तुएँ
questions.explanation: Public goods are available to all (non-excludable) and one person's use doesn't prevent others from using them (non-rival). While governments often provide them, they are defined by these characteristics, not by their producer.

questions.question: Banking, transport, and teaching are all examples of which economic sector? / बैंकिंग, परिवहन और शिक्षण, ये सभी किस आर्थिक क्षेत्र के उदाहरण हैं? DP - 2025
questions.explanation: The Tertiary Sector is also known as the service sector. It involves the provision of services rather than end products. Banking, transport, teaching, retail, and hospitality are all classic examples of service-based economic activities.

questions.question: Which economic sector is also known as the 'service sector'? / किस आर्थिक क्षेत्र को 'सेवा क्षेत्र' के नाम से भी जाना जाता है? DP - 2025
questions.explanation: The Tertiary sector is synonymous with the service sector. This sector provides services to the general population and to businesses, including retail, transportation, entertainment, restaurants, and clerical services.

questions.question: What is the term for the act of selling domestic goods and services to foreign countries? / घरेलु वस्तुओं और सेवाओं को विदेशी देशों को बेचने की प्रक्रिया को क्या कहते हैं? DP - 2025
questions.explanation: Exports are goods and services produced in one country and sold to buyers in another. Imports are the reverse: goods and services bought from foreign countries. A trade deficit occurs when a country's imports exceed its exports.

questions.question: Which economic sector is alternatively known as the 'agricultural and related sector'? / किस आर्थिक क्षेत्र को 'कृषि और संबंधित क्षेत्र' के नाम से भी जाना जाता है? DP - 2025
questions.explanation: The primary sector involves the extraction and harvesting of natural products from the earth. Since agriculture is the most significant activity in this sector, it is often called the 'agricultural and related sector,' which also includes forestry, fishing, and mining.

questions.question: Which is an example of the 'primary sector' of the economy? / अर्थव्यवस्था के 'प्राथमिक क्षेत्र' का उदाहरण कौन सा है? DP - 2025
questions.explanation: The primary sector is based on the extraction of raw materials. Farming directly involves cultivating land to produce crops, making it a core primary sector activity. A car factory is secondary, while a bank and software company are tertiary/quaternary.

questions.question: In the context of public goods, what does 'Non-rivalrous' stand for? / सार्वजनिक वस्तुओं के संदर्भ में, "गैर-प्रतिस्पर्धी" का क्या अर्थ है? SSC CHSL 2025
options: One person's use reduces another's use / एक व्यक्ति के उपयोग से दूसरे व्यक्ति के उपयोग में कमी आती है।
One person's use doesn't reduce another's use / एक व्यक्ति के उपयोग से दूसरे व्यक्ति के उपयोग में कमी नहीं आती।
The good is a Giffen good / यह वस्तु गिफेन मानक के अंतर्गत आती है।
The good is only available for bureaucrats / यह वस्तु केवल नौकरशाहों के लिए उपलब्ध है।
questions.explanation: "Non-rivalrous" (or non-rivalry) means that the consumption of a good by one individual does not reduce the availability of that good for consumption by others. For example, one person benefiting from national defense does not prevent another from also benefiting.

questions.question: The _ sector of an economy involves group of activities related with extraction of raw materials directly from nature. / अर्थव्यवस्था का _ क्षेत्र प्रकृति से सीधे कच्चे माल के निष्कर्षण से संबंधित गतिविधियों के समूह को दर्शाता है। SSC CHSL 2025
questions.explanation: The primary sector of the economy is defined by its direct use of natural resources. This includes agriculture, forestry, fishing, and mining, all of which involve extracting raw materials directly from the natural environment.

questions.question: The extraction of oil from groundnut is an example of economic activity of which sector of economy? / मूंगफली से तेल निकालना अर्थव्यवस्था के किस क्षेत्र की आर्थिक गतिविधि का उदाहरण है? SSC CHSL 2025
questions.explanation: Groundnut is a product of the primary sector (agriculture). Extracting oil from it is a processing/manufacturing activity, which transforms a raw material into a more valuable product. Therefore, it belongs to the secondary sector.

questions.question: In the context of economy, which of the following is an example of Intermediate good? / अर्थव्यवस्था के संदर्भ में, निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा मध्यवर्ती वस्तु का उदाहरण है? SSC CHSL 2025
options: Copper used for making utensils / बर्तन बनाने के लिए प्रयुक्त तांबा
Television purchase by household / घरेलू उपयोग द्वारा खरीदा गया टेलीविजन
questions.explanation: An intermediate good is a product used to produce a final good or finished product. Copper used to make utensils is an input in the manufacturing process. A shirt, TV, or refrigerator purchased by a consumer is a final good.

questions.question: 'This sector of economic activities involves processing of raw materials derived from primary sector into products for sale or consumption.' Which sector of the economy is being talked about in the information given above? / 'आर्थिक गतिविधियों के इस क्षेत्र में प्राथमिक क्षेत्र से प्राप्त कच्चे माल को बिक्री या उपभोग के लिए उत्पादों में परिवर्तित करना शामिल है।' दी गई जानकारी में अर्थव्यवस्था के किस क्षेत्र की बात हो रही है? SSC CHSL 2025
questions.explanation: This is the definition of the Secondary Sector. It takes raw materials from the Primary Sector (like wood, cotton, or iron ore) and processes them into finished goods (like furniture, clothes, or steel) through manufacturing.

questions.question: Which of the following is also known as a "Centrally Planned Economy"? / निम्नलिखित में से किसे "केंद्रीकृत नियोजित अर्थव्यवस्था" के रूप में भी जाना जाता है? SSC CPO - 2025
options: Free market economy / मुक्त बाज़ार अर्थव्यवस्था
questions.explanation: A centrally planned economy, also known as a command economy, is a system where the government makes all economic decisions regarding production and distribution. This is the defining characteristic of a socialist economic system. A free market economy is another term for a capitalist economy.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT within the scope of macroeconomics? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा मैक्रोइकॉनॉमिक्स के दायरे में नहीं आता है? SSC CPO - 2025
options: Wages of an establishment / संस्थान का वेतन
questions.explanation: Macroeconomics studies the economy as a whole, focusing on aggregate variables like national income, total investment, and total consumption. The wages of a single establishment (firm) is a topic for microeconomics, which studies the economic behavior of individual units like households and firms.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a macroeconomic indicator? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा एक व्यापक आर्थिक संकेतक का उदाहरण है? SSC PHASE XIII 28/07/2025 - MORNING
options: Price of a specific product / किसी विशिष्ट उत्पाद की कीमत
National unemployment rate / राष्ट्रीय बेरोजगारी दर
Demand for a particular brand / किसी विशेष ब्रांड की मांग
Cost of production for a company / किसी कंपनी की उत्पादन लागत
questions.explanation: Macroeconomic indicators measure the economy as a whole. The national unemployment rate is an aggregate measure for the entire country. The price of a specific product, demand for a brand, and a single company's costs are all microeconomic concepts.

questions.question: In economics, which of the following concepts is defined over a period of time? / अर्थशास्त्र में, निम्नलिखित में से कौन सी अवधारणा समय की अवधि में परिभाषित होती है? SSC GD 17/02/2025 EVENING
options: Both Flow and Stock / प्रवाह और स्टॉक दोनों
Neither Flow nor Stock / न तो प्रवाह और न ही स्टॉक
questions.explanation: A flow variable is measured over an interval of time (e.g., income per year, investment per quarter). A stock variable is measured at one specific point in time (e.g., wealth on a specific date). The question asks for what is defined "over a period of time," which is the definition of a flow.

questions.question: Which of the following sectors includes all the business entities, corporations and partnerships? / निम्नलिखित में से किस क्षेत्र में सभी व्यावसायिक संस्थाएं, निगम और साझेदारी शामिल हैं? SSC GD 07/02/2025 AFTERNOON
questions.explanation: In the circular flow of income model, the 'Firms sector' is the aggregate of all producing units in the economy. This includes all forms of business entities that produce goods and services.

questions.question: What does the term 'commerce' mainly refer to? / 'वाणिज्य' शब्द का मुख्य रूप से क्या अर्थ है? SSC PHASE XIII 10/02/2025 EVENING
options: Production of goods / वस्तुओं का उत्पादन
Distribution of goods / वस्तुओं का वितरण
Manufacturing of goods / वस्तुओं का निर्माण
questions.explanation: Commerce refers to the activities that facilitate the exchange and distribution of goods from the producer to the final consumer. It includes trade and aids to trade like transport, banking, and advertising, but not the production itself.

questions.question: From where does the customer get the ability to buy goods and services? / ग्राहक को सामान और सेवाएं खरीदने की क्षमता कहां से मिलती है? SSC PHASE XIII 17/02/2025 MORNING
options: Taste and preference / रुचि और पसंद
questions.explanation: While advertisement and preferences influence the *desire* to buy, the fundamental *ability* or purchasing power to acquire goods and services comes from a customer's income or wealth.

questions.question: Which sectors should work together to be known as a mixed economy? / मिश्रित अर्थव्यवस्था कहलाने के लिए किन क्षेत्रों को एक साथ मिलकर काम करना चाहिए? SSC PHASE XIII 11/02/2025 EVENING
options: Private and Corporate / निजी और कॉर्पोरेट
Government and Cooperative / सरकारी और सहकारी
Foreign and Private / विदेशी और निजी
Government and Private / सरकारी और निजी
questions.explanation: A mixed economy is defined by the co-existence of both the government (public sector) and private individuals/companies (private sector) in the allocation of resources and ownership of production. India is a prime example.

questions.question: Which of the following is a part of the Primary Sector of the Indian Economy? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन भारत की अर्थव्यवस्था के प्राथमिक क्षेत्र का हिस्सा है? SSC GD 12/02/2025 AFTERNOON
questions.explanation: The primary sector deals with extracting natural resources. Animal husbandry (raising livestock) is an activity directly related to agriculture and nature. Broadcasting and real estate are tertiary, while construction is secondary.

questions.question: What does the term 'capital formation' refer to in macroeconomics? / वृहद अर्थशास्त्र में 'पूंजी निर्माण' शब्द का क्या अर्थ है? SSC STENO 06/08/2025 Shift-3
options: Growth in production capacity through investment / निवेश के माध्यम से उत्पादन क्षमता में वृद्धि
Decrease in foreign direct investment / प्रत्यक्ष विदेशी निवेश में कमी
Expansion of consumer credit / उपभोक्ता ऋण का विस्तार
Increase in financial savings / वित्तीय बचत में वृद्धि
questions.explanation: Capital formation is a term used to describe the net capital accumulation during an accounting period. It refers to the addition to the stock of capital goods (like machinery and equipment) in the economy, which is achieved through investment and increases the overall production capacity.

questions.question: Which of the following reflects the 'what to produce' decision in economics? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा विकल्प अर्थशास्त्र में 'क्या उत्पादन करें' के निर्णय को दर्शाता है? SSC STENO 08/08/2025 Shift-3
options: Choosing between capital and consumer goods / पूंजी और उपभोक्ता वस्तुओं के बीच चयन
Increasing taxes to raise government revenue / सरकारी राजस्व बढ़ाने के लिए करों में वृद्धि
Deciding on distribution of output / उत्पादन का वितरण पर निर्णय
Choosing method of production / उत्पादन विधि का चयन
questions.explanation: The fundamental economic problem includes three questions: what, how, and for whom to produce. 'What to produce' involves deciding the mix of goods and services an economy will create, such as choosing between producing more capital goods (for future growth) or more consumer goods (for current satisfaction).

questions.question: Which of the following sectors includes banking, communication and transport? / निम्नलिखित में से किस क्षेत्र में बैंकिंग, संचार और परिवहन शामिल हैं? RRB NTPC UG 20/08/2025 - AFTERNOON
questions.explanation: Banking, communication, and transport are all services. The economic sector that deals with providing services is known as the Tertiary sector or the service sector.

questions.question: Tourism in India contributes to which type of economic activity? / भारत में पर्यटन किस प्रकार की आर्थिक गतिविधि में योगदान देता है? RRB NTPC UG 29/08/2025 - EVENING
questions.explanation: Tourism is a service-based industry. It provides services like hospitality, transportation, and entertainment to tourists. Therefore, it is a part of the Tertiary (service) sector of the economy.

questions.question: The term 'License Raj' is related to which sector of the Indian economy? / 'लाइसेंस राज' शब्द भारतीय अर्थव्यवस्था के किस क्षेत्र से संबंधित है? RRB NTPC UG 11/08/2025 - EVENING
questions.explanation: The term 'License Raj' refers to the elaborate system of licenses, regulations, and accompanying red tape that were required to set up and run businesses in India between the 1950s and 1991. This system primarily affected the Industrial sector.

questions.question: Which of the following is a part of the secondary sector in India? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा भारत में द्वितीयक क्षेत्र का हिस्सा है? RRB NTPC 17/06/2025 AFTERNOON
options: Weaving cotton fabric / सूती कपड़े की बुनाई
questions.explanation: The secondary sector involves manufacturing. Weaving cotton fabric is a manufacturing process that converts a raw material (cotton) into a product. Fishing and farming are primary, and teaching is a tertiary activity.

questions.question: Economic liberalisation mainly affects which sector? / आर्थिक उदारीकरण मुख्य रूप से किस क्षेत्र को प्रभावित करता है? SSC CGL 2025
options: Primary and Tertiary / प्राथमिक और तृतीयक
Primary and Secondary / प्राथमिक और माध्यमिक
Secondary and Tertiary / माध्यमिक और तृतीयक
Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary / प्राथमिक, माध्यमिक और तृतीयक
questions.explanation: Economic liberalization, like the LPG reforms of 1991 in India, involves reducing government regulations, encouraging private participation, and opening up the economy. These reforms have a wide-ranging impact across all sectors - agriculture (Primary), industry (Secondary), and services (Tertiary).
